defs: A variant of Fere, a mate, a companion.

records.ety: [OE. fer, feer, fere, AS. fǣr a coming suddenly upon, fear, danger; akin to D. vaar, OHG. fāra danger, G. gefahr, Icel. fār harm, mischief, plague, and to E. fare, peril. See Fare.]

defs: A painful emotion or passion excited by the expectation of evil, or the apprehension of impending danger; apprehension; anxiety; solicitude; alarm; dread.
quotes: Fear is an uneasiness of the mind, upon the thought of future evil likely to befall us.
Where no hope is left, is left no fear.
notes: ☞ The degrees of this passion, beginning with the most moderate, may be thus expressed, -- apprehension, fear, dread, fright, terror.

defs: Apprehension of incurring, or solicitude to avoid, God's wrath; the trembling and awful reverence felt toward the Supreme Being.
Respectful reverence for men of authority or worth.
quotes: I will put my fear in their hearts.
I will teach you the fear of the Lord.
Render therefore to all their dues; tribute to whom tribute is due . . . fear to whom fear.

defs: That which causes, or which is the object of, apprehension or alarm; source or occasion of terror; danger; dreadfulness.
quotes: There were they in great fear, where no fear was.
The fear of your adventure would counsel you to a more equal enterprise.

records.ety: [OE. feren, faeren, to frighten, to be afraid, AS. fǣran to terrify. See Fear, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Feared (fērd); p. pr. & vb. n. Fearing.]

defs: To feel a painful apprehension of; to be afraid of; to consider or expect with emotion of alarm or solicitude.
quotes: I will fear no evil, for thou art with me.
I greatly fear my money is not safe.
I almost fear to quit your hand.
notes: With subordinate clause. I greatly fear my money is not safe. Shak. I almost fear to quit your hand. D. Jerrold.

defs: To have a reverential awe of; to be solicitous to avoid the displeasure of.
quotes: Leave them to God above; him serve and fear.

defs: To be anxious or solicitous for; now replaced by fear for.
quotes: The sins of the father are to be laid upon the children, therefore . . . I fear you.
Ay what else, fear you not her courage?

defs: To affright; to terrify; to drive away or prevent approach of by fear.
quotes: Fear their people from doing evil.
Tush, tush! fear boys with bugs.

defs: To be in apprehension of evil; to be afraid; to feel anxiety on account of some expected evil.
